title: [Aerodactyl] Battle Guide
summary: A 130 Speed [Rock]/[Flying] [lead] that guarantees [stealth-rock] and [taunt] both go up on turn one, denying oppo[sing] setup plans.
strengths: 130 Speed wins essentially every [lead] race in the format
weaknesses: 80/65/75 bulk folds to fast special attackers
Only 105 Attack — damage output is [lead]-tier, not breaker-tier

recommendedMoves.reason: Lead-defining hazard setup for team pressure

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Aerodactyl] is a [Rock]/[Flying] [lead] whose entire design is built a[round] winning the opening turn of the game. 130 base Speed sits near the top of the format, which means [Aerodactyl] goes first in almost every [lead] mirror, and from there the plan is simple. [Aerodactyl] isn't a breaker or a cleaner, but the tempo it seizes on turn one routinely snowballs into a won mid-game.

## Strengths
The Speed tier is the [sing]le most important [strength]. Very few unboosted Pokemon outpace 130 base, so [Aerodactyl] reliably moves first in the all-important opening turn. [taunt] removes the threat of oppo[sing] hazard [lead]s, status spreaders, and [defog] users — the most common turn-one responses [Aerodactyl] would otherwise face. [unnerve] is a minor but nonzero bonus that [disable]s the oppo[sing] Pokemon's berry trigger while [Aerodactyl] is on the field.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Aerodactyl]'s 80/65/75 bulk is thin enough that any fast special attacker removes it before the second turn. It's weak to [Ice], [Electric], [Rock], [Steel], and [Water], which means the natural answer pool is large. Damage output is only 105 Attack, which means [Aerodactyl] can't muscle through walls and has to rely on its utility moves.

## Recommended Moveset
[stealth-rock] is the locked utility slot because that's the reason [Aerodactyl] exists in the lineup. [taunt] is mandatory to prevent the oppo[sing] [lead] from setting its own hazards or status spreading, and the order of those two moves is usually [taunt] first, [rock]s second. [stone-edge] is the [Rock] STAB finisher for when [Aerodactyl] needs to damage something, and [earthquake] covers [Steel] and [Electric] [pivot]s that try to [absorb] the [Rock] move.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[focus-sash] is the standard [lead] item. [sharp-beak] is an alternate choice for the rare offensive build that wants [brave-bird] or [acrobatics] as the [Flying] STAB instead of the utility plan. The ability should be [unnerve] for the berry shutdown; [rock]-head and pressure are narrow alternatives. Tera [Rock] maxes the [stone-edge] damage, while Tera [Flying] erases the [Ground] weakness and sets up a cleaner follow-up.

## Teammates & Synergy
The whole point of [lead]ing [Aerodactyl] is to build tempo for a follow-up breaker. Hazard synergy is valuable: a second hazard setter ([spikes] or [toxic-spikes]) com[pound]s the chip damage that [stealth-rock] starts. A fast [pivot] lets [Aerodactyl] retreat safely after its [lead] turn, and a special wall on the back line covers the biggest defensive weakness left behind.

## Play Patterns
Turn one [Aerodactyl]'s play is almost scripted. Turn two use whichever move wasn't used on turn one, then [pivot] out before special attackers remove it. [stone-edge] and [earthquake] are finishers for weakened opponents later in the match when [Aerodactyl] enters as a cleanup [pivot]. Save the Tera for a specific defensive swing you can't survive otherwise.
